untranslatable_reason: Signal requires multi-timeframe analysis (weekly overextension vs daily conviction) which cannot be expressed in single-timeframe indicator logic. 'Weekly is overextended' and 'Daily shows lack of conviction at extremes' require explicit weekly/daily timeframe comparison operators not available in the engine. Translated entry conditions as proxies: RSI extremes (overextension), low body_pct (indecision), declining volume, and no new highs. This is a best-effort approximation only.
translation_notes: CRITICAL: This signal fundamentally requires multi-timeframe logic that cannot be properly expressed in a single-timeframe backtest engine. The original rule compares weekly chart extremes against daily price action indecision—this requires simultaneous analysis of two timeframe contexts. Translated using: (1) RSI >70/<30 as proxy for 'overextended on higher timeframe', (2) body_pct <0.3 for 'indecisive/lack of conviction', (3) volume < hvol_20 for 'declining volume on final push', (4) no new highs/lows vs previous bar. Exit uses sma_20 crossover as mean reversion trigger. Recommend implementing with explicit dual-timeframe rules in backtest engine for accuracy.
